stories.body: U.S. Central Command said it carried out back-to-back “self-defense strikes” on Iranian military, surveillance, and radar sites in southern Iran after President Donald Trump warned that Tehran would be hit “hard,” a move that triggered retaliatory missile attacks on U.S. bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan, fresh claims by Iran’s Revolutionary Guard Corps that it destroyed assets at Jordan’s Muwaffaq Salti Airbase, and dueling narratives over whether commercial traffic is moving through the Strait of Hormuz even as oil prices spike toward $95 a barrel and Iranian state media report hits on oil tankers.
stories.headline: US and Iran trade fresh strikes as ceasefire efforts unravel across the Middle East

stories.body: Iranian officials intensified demands that Israel withdraw from southern Lebanon as a condition for any broader ceasefire with the United States, with Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi and senior adviser Mohsen Rezaei underscoring Hezbollah’s role in the conflict, warning Israel against further strikes near Beirut, and tying maritime tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and U.S. tanker boardings to stalled negotiations aimed at unlocking Iranian oil revenues and easing sanctions.
stories.headline: Tehran links any regional deal to Israeli withdrawal while doubling down on support for Hezbollah

stories.body: A sudden doubling of an air leak aboard the International Space Station prompted NASA to order five astronauts into their Crew Dragon spacecraft for about two hours before reversing the evacuation when Roscosmos paused disputed repair work on a crack in the Zvezda module, highlighting months-long disagreements over leak diagnostics, emergency procedures, and a rare safe-haven command that underscores the political stakes of extending the station’s life to 2032.
stories.headline: NASA orders, then halts, ISS evacuation after air leak scare amid disputes with Roscosmos

stories.body: Kyiv officials urged residents into shelters as missiles sparked fires across several districts, including a nine-story building in Podil and car fires in Obolon, amid Ukrainian warnings of a potential massive Russian strike following Moscow’s vow to target command centers in response to alleged attacks in occupied Luhansk.
stories.headline: Kyiv endures one of the heaviest Russian barrages in weeks

stories.body: Northern Ireland police used water cannon at Sandyknowes roundabout near Belfast as masked crowds hurled bricks, petrol bombs, and debris after a Sudanese refugee was charged in a knife attack that left Stephen Ogilvie severely injured, with ministers blaming far-right agitators for spreading racist misinformation while protests and sporadic riots forced businesses to close and public transport to shut down.
stories.headline: Belfast unrest intensifies after knife attack, prompting water cannon deployments

stories.body: The Senate voted 52-47 to approve an additional $70 billion for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol over three years while fending off bipartisan attempts to permanently bar President Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, leaving Democrats warning about an unchecked slush fund even as Republicans cite acting Attorney General Todd Blanche’s assurances that it will remain dormant ahead of House consideration next week.
stories.headline: Senate passes $70 billion ICE funding bill while rejecting bid to kill Trump’s 'anti-weaponization' fund

stories.body: A fresh POLITICO/Public First analysis shows majorities in the United States and United Kingdom believe politicians serve themselves, with 71 percent of U.S. adults saying officials look out only for themselves and 64 percent of Britons doubting Prime Minister Keir Starmer will last the year, signaling a persistent anti-incumbent mood that now threatens President Trump’s midterm prospects as cynical voters focus on cost-of-living strains and foreign entanglements.
stories.headline: New POLITICO poll finds deep voter cynicism still threatening incumbents on both sides of the Atlantic

stories.body: Democrats are bitterly divided over Maine Senate hopeful Graham Platner after a New York Times report detailed allegations from ex-girlfriends of physical aggression and knowledge of a Nazi-like tattoo, compounding prior sexting and offensive Reddit controversies even as party strategists say defeating Sen. Susan Collins is impossible without him and progressive allies like Rep. Ro Khanna continue to campaign by his side.
stories.headline: Democrats split over Maine’s Graham Platner after new allegations roil must-win Senate race

stories.body: Rep. Nancy Mace failed to make the South Carolina gubernatorial runoff after scandals and her role in releasing the Jeffrey Epstein files alienated President Trump, clearing the way for Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette and Attorney General Alan Wilson to advance while leaving Mace without her House seat and potentially free to antagonize Trump’s legislative agenda during her remaining months in Congress.
stories.headline: Nancy Mace loses South Carolina GOP gubernatorial primary after clashes with Trump

stories.body: Sen. Lindsey Graham and aligned super PACs have poured more than $18 million into ads to fend off businessman Mark Lynch and other challengers who accuse the incumbent of abandoning Trump’s original anti-interventionist “America First” ideals by championing the war with Iran, setting up a proxy fight over the GOP’s direction despite Trump’s endorsement of Graham ahead of Tuesday’s primary.
stories.headline: Lindsey Graham faces well-funded 'America First' challenge as he tries to avoid runoff

stories.body: Pope León XIV will join Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez on a visit to the Arguineguín pier in Gran Canaria, the so-called “dock of shame” where nearly 3,000 migrants were left in squalid outdoor conditions during 2020’s migration surge, a trip meant to highlight past rights abuses and the Spanish state’s slow response as authorities struggled with pandemic-era arrivals across the Atlantic route.
stories.headline: Pope León XIV takes migration-focused trip from Barcelona to the Canary Islands’ 'dock of shame'

stories.body: Former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero’s lawyer asked Spain’s National Court to request detailed documentation from U.S. authorities about how Homeland Security Investigations seized and cloned Plus Ultra executive Rodolfo Reyes’s phone in 2021, arguing that gaps in the chain of custody and judicial authorization jeopardize Zapatero’s defense against allegations he influenced a €53 million airline bailout.
stories.headline: Zapatero presses judge to clarify US role in Plus Ultra phone evidence amid corruption probe

stories.body: Pope León XIV blessed the Sagrada Família’s newly completed Tower of Jesus before 120,000 onlookers during a two-day Barcelona visit that also included social outreach at Sant Agustí parish, a vigil at the Olympic Stadium, meetings at Brians 1 prison, and a Montserrat pilgrimage, all under an extensive security operation that disrupted mobility across the Eixample.
stories.headline: Massive crowds and tight security as Pope blesses the Sagrada Família’s new tower

stories.body: Catalan police are hunting a gunman who allegedly shot a man in the head outside the National Police’s DNI office on Carrer Balmes around 10 a.m., a brazen attack captured on security cameras during the citywide lockdown for the papal visit and just days after another fatal shooting in Zona Franca, prompting local politicians to demand tougher action against organized crime and illegal arms.
stories.headline: Mossos probe fatal daylight shooting on Carrer Balmes outside national police station

stories.body: Curator and writer Valentín Roma, dismissed from MACBA in 2015 after the censorship row over “La bèstia i el sobirà,” has been chosen to lead the museum for five years following his tenure revitalizing La Virreina Centre de la Imatge, though arts associations and the museum’s works council criticize the recruitment process for lacking transparency even as the jury praised Roma’s intellectual rigor and vision.
stories.headline: Valentín Roma returns to direct MACBA amid controversy over the selection process
